The bidirectional import relationship with hench-agent-engine is bounded and intentional: guard wraps agent tools, agent references guard policy. This is preferable to a pure unidirectional dependency which would require the agent to be unaware of its own guard.
Good cohesion (0.8) and low coupling (0.2) confirm the guard layer maintains a clean separation of concerns — policy logic is not entangled with agent execution logic.

Two entry points (guard/index.ts and guard/paths.ts) expose the guard API surface. Exporting paths.ts directly alongside the index barrel creates a dual-entry risk — consumers may bypass index.ts and import paths.ts directly, undermining the intended abstraction boundary.

Cross-zone import flow is directionally clean: satellites import from the engine (not each other), and the engine has minimal upward dependencies — the topology is correct even if individual satellite boundaries need tightening.

The web package's four internal zones (web-server-api, web-dashboard-viewer, viewer-message-pipeline, web-landing-page) form a clear hub topology with web-dashboard-viewer at the center — 84 imports from web-server-api into web-dashboard-viewer confirms the viewer is the dominant composition surface.

The bidirectional import relationship between viewer-message-pipeline (web) and web-dashboard-viewer (web-viewer) — 15 outbound + 12 inbound — is the single highest architectural risk in this batch; it suggests the messaging layer has not fully separated from the viewer hub and may be accumulating mixed responsibilities.

Sourcevision Analysis Engine's perfect cohesion and zero coupling stand in sharp contrast to the web package's internal fragmentation, reinforcing that domain packages with a single gateway entry point are the healthiest structural pattern in this monorepo.

web-viewer is the monorepo's dominant hub: it participates in 6 of the 15 listed cross-zone import edges (84 outbound to web-server, 15 inbound from web, 12 outbound to web, 3 outbound to project-status-polling, 2 each with crash). This level of centrality is expected for a UI hub but should be monitored — adding further import edges to web-viewer increases blast radius for any refactoring.

The project-status-polling zone (use) forms a bidirectional loop with web-viewer (use → web-viewer: 2, web-viewer → use: 3). Bidirectional zone dependencies are an architectural smell; this zone is a strong candidate for dissolution into web-viewer to eliminate the cycle.

All three test-only zones (web-integration-tests, monorepo-e2e-tests, monorepo-integration-tests) have perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling, confirming that the test organization is clean and tests are not leaking into the production import graph.

The crash zone's bidirectional coupling with web-viewer is the only documented architectural exception to the hub topology rule; it is controlled and policy-enforced. All other bidirectional edges (notably use ↔ web-viewer) are undocumented and should be resolved.

The cross-zone import counts reveal a clear asymmetry: web-viewer → web-server has 84 imports while web-server → web-viewer has only 5, correctly reflecting the composition-root pattern where the server wires the viewer as a static asset rather than importing it at runtime.

Foundation packages (llm-client, root orchestration) correctly show zero outbound coupling, validating the four-tier dependency hierarchy. Any future addition that introduces imports from these tiers into domain packages should be flagged as an architectural violation.

The hench-agent-engine zone at 141 files is approaching the scale where sub-zone boundary drift becomes a risk. Adding automated boundary assertions (similar to web/boundary-check.test.ts) before it reaches web-viewer's 270-file scale would prevent gradual erosion.

Zone cohesion metrics for zones containing only test files (or predominantly test files) are structurally biased toward 0 because test files rarely import each other. The analysis pipeline should weight test-zone cohesion differently or exclude test-only zones from cohesion health reporting.

Three of five Rex zones fall into dual-fragility territory; the common pattern is a 2-4 file satellite with high outbound coupling to rex-prd-engine — a planned absorption pass would consolidate these into the engine and eliminate three warning-level zones in one PR.

The rex-prd-engine's 216-file scale with cohesion 0.98 is architecturally admirable, but at this size internal sub-zone documentation (similar to hench-agent's sub-zone governance in CLAUDE.md) would reduce onboarding friction and make future decomposition easier.

All five zones in this batch have cohesion at or above 0.56 — none fall into the dual-fragility (<0.4 cohesion AND >0.6 coupling) risk zone, which is a positive signal compared to rex-cli and prd-fix-command in previous batches.

The viewer-message-pipeline ↔ web-dashboard-viewer bidirectional import cycle (27 total cross-edges) is the only structural concern in this batch and should be resolved by enforcing strict directionality: messaging imports viewer types but viewer never imports messaging internals directly.

Web-landing-page's total isolation (no consumers, no imports) suggests it may be wired via build artifact rather than module graph — confirm this is intentional and document the wiring mechanism to prevent future contributors from adding incorrect runtime imports.

The monorepo has three separate test zones at the root level (monorepo-e2e-tests, monorepo-integration-tests) plus package-level test zones (web-integration-tests, rex-e2e). This layered test strategy is intentional and provides good coverage at each architectural seam — preserve the separation rather than consolidating all tests into one location.

The project-status-polling zone (use) meets both dual-fragility thresholds (cohesion 0.25, coupling 0.75) and has a bidirectional dependency with web-viewer. It should be dissolved into web-viewer's hooks/ directory to eliminate the artificial zone boundary and the import cycle.

Gateway modules (hench/src/prd/rex-gateway.ts, web/src/server/rex-gateway.ts, web/src/viewer/external.ts) are the sole cross-package import surfaces and correctly appear as high-weight edges in the cross-zone import counts — this confirms the gateway pattern is structurally enforced, not just documented.
packages/rex/tests/e2e/ contains files from 3 zones (rex-cli-e2e-coverage, rex-package-e2e-tests, rex-prd-engine) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries

packages/web/tests/unit/server/ contains files from 4 zones (rex-prd-engine, sourcevision-analysis-engine, web-dashboard-viewer, web-server-api) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries
